page: UpCloud PREMIUM-2xCPU-8GB is a virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor, featuring an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor. It is configured with 2 shared vCPUs, consisting of 2 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 100 GB SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

Benchmark data reveals a mixed performance profile. Single-core CPU performance is highly competitive, with stress-ng single-core, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all ranking in the top tier (top 10%). However, multi-core and multi-threaded operations show limitations, likely due to the shared CPU allocation. Multi-core stress-ng performance, static web serving throughput, and small-model LLM inference speeds (135M model) fall into the average tier. Conversely, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, PassMark CPU scores, and Redis SET operations are categorized as weak (bottom 25%), while PassMark memory scores and medium-model (7B) LLM text generation are rated as poor (bottom 10%).

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers a balanced resource density of 4.0 GB RAM per vCPU alongside bundled SSD storage, making it a viable option for entry-level workloads. However, the shared CPU allocation introduces performance tradeoffs, particularly for memory-intensive or sustained multi-threaded tasks, as evidenced by the lower PassMark memory and database operation scores. This configuration is best suited for workloads such as lightweight web serving, basic single-threaded processing tasks, and small-scale application hosting where peak multi-core performance is not continuously required.
description: UpCloud PREMIUM-2xCPU-8GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 2 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor on the x86_64 architecture, paired with 8.0 GB of RAM and 100 GB of local SSD storage. While single-core benchmarks like stress-ng and single-threaded compression rank in the top 10%, multi-core and memory-bound performance metrics, such as PassMark memory scores and Redis SET operations, fall into the weak or poor categories. This performance profile reflects the tradeoffs of its shared CPU allocation. The server is qualitatively cost-efficient for entry-level hosting, making it suitable for lightweight web serving, single-threaded utility scripts, and small-scale application development. It also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AES cryptography.
og_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-2xCPU-8GB is a KVM virtual server with 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 100 GB SSD storage. It delivers strong single-core performance alongside average web serving capabilities.
meta_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-2xCPU-8GB is a KVM virtual server featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and a bundled 100 GB SSD for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A shared-vCPU virtual server combining strong single-core processing with bundled SSD storage for lightweight general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 2 shared AMD EPYC 9575F vCPUs and 8.0 GB RAM
Top-tier single-core CPU and compression performance
Includes one complimentary public IPv4 address
Best suited for lightweight web serving and single-threaded tasks
